title: contributing when self employed
body:
O.P.
Looking at the typical contribution options available in funds like AustralianSuper; you can advise your employer of your fund, you can make a concessional contribution (immediately taxed by fund at 15%) or you can make a non-concessional contribution (not taxed by fund. taxed in pay).
What is the recommended method when you are a sole trader making your own pre-tax contributions? The concessional sees an immediate 15% tax. Alternatively, should the person describe it as non-concessional – so that no immediate tax is applied – and then take care of the tax with the ATO when tax time comes? The advantage being that no tax is paid until much later.
